channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KREX 5

item.title: Greenland's leader says the island 'is ours' as Trump vows to acquire the territory
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Greenland's prime minister declared Wednesday that “Greenland is ours” and cannot be taken or bought in defiance of a message from U.S.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israel’s supply cutoff to Gaza sends prices soaring as aid groups race to distribute scarce stocks
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s cutoff of food, fuel, medicine and other supplies to Gaza’s 2 million people has sent prices soaring and humanitarian groups into overdrive trying to distribute dwindling stocks to the most vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pope resting after sleeping through the night with ventilation mask
item.description: Pope Francis remained in stable condition, with a guarded prognosis, meaning he was not out of danger.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams, an ex-cop, has 'upper hand' over other sanctuary city mayors: Blue Lives Matter founder
item.description: NYC Mayor Eric Adams has a leg up over his fellow sanctuary city leaders ahead of their House testimony, according to the founder of a pro-police nonprofit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Five takeaways from Trump's address to Congress
item.description: President Trump delivered the first congressional address of his second term on Tuesday evening. It was a chance for the president to underscore the sweeping changes he has wrought in the six weeks since he returned to the White House – and to defend those moves from his many critics.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The US is killing someone by firing squad for the 1st time in 15 years. Here's a look at the history
item.description: It was a punishment for mutiny in colonial times, a way to discourage desertion during the Civil War and a dose of frontier justice in the Old West. In modern times, some consider it a more humane alternative to lethal injection. The firing squad has a long and thorny history in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Powerful US storms kill 2 and bring threats from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms killed two people in Mississippi, tore the roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in a small town in Oklahoma and threatened more communities across the nation Tuesday with wide-ranging weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump: 'A new president' has solved the border crisis
item.description: The president said that a change in leadership, not new legislation, was the key to controlling border crossings.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration releases list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it has identified to close or sell, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Joann store closures: See the list of nearly 800 locations headed for sale
item.description: After more than 80 years, national fabric and craft retailer Joann is another step closer to being erased from shopping centers across the U.S. after the release of nearly 800 locations going up for sale in the coming months.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why Trump's speech to Congress isn't a 'State of the Union' address
item.description: It looks like the State of the Union, and will be carried on live television, just like those annual addresses are. But it's called something else: a joint address to Congress. And it has its origins in the first term of President Ronald Reagan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump wants to use the 'God Squad' to increase logging, but it must follow strict rules
item.description: President Donald Trump wants to increase logging in national forests and on public lands, including by bypassing endangered species protections.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer mice with thick hair like the extinct woolly mammoth
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Extinction is still forever, but scientists at the biotech company Colossal Biosciences are trying what they say is the next best thing to restoring ancient beasts — genetically engineering living animals with qualities to resemble extinct species like the woolly mammoth.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Diddy, Luigi Mangione in partial lockdown at prison after 'race riot': Report
item.description: The Metropolitan Detention Center, where Sean "Diddy" Combs and Luigi Mangione are being held, is partially locked down due to a "race riot.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Royal Caribbean cruise passenger presumed dead after going overboard on ‘80s-themed voyage, family says
item.description: A woman is presumed dead after going overboard on a Royal Caribbean ship during the first day of the '80s-themed cruise near the first port in the Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Restaurants owners who support tipped wage bill were doxxed: Dems
item.description: "We want to make it clear that these actions are unacceptable," Speaker Julie McCluskie and House Majority Leader Monica Duran said.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia daycare owner speaks after toddler wanders from facility
item.description: Monique Ede, owner of Fortress of God Childcare in Chester has spoken exclusively with 8News after a child wandered off from her facility.
item.dc:creator: Rolynn Wilson, NewsNation

item.title: IHOP offers free short stack for National Pancake Day
item.description: Calling all pancake lovers! If you are ready to satisfy your craving, IHOP is celebrating National Pancake Day on March 4 with a free treat.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, KRON4

item.title: Red Robin considering closing dozens of 'underperforming' restaurants
item.description: Red Robin, which has locations in all but five states, says it's reviewing dozens of "underperforming" stores for potential closure.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Target CEO: Customers will 'likely see price increases' after Trump tariffs
item.description: The tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump on Tuesday are likely to have a direct impact on the wallets of Americans, a major CEO said. In an appearance on CNBC on Tuesday, Target CEO Brian Cornell said the company will probably raise prices on produce and fruits as early as this week.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: February 'lowest in history' for border encounters, White House says
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem says February was the "lowest month in recorded history for (migrant) encounters at our border.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: SDSU researchers ramp up border sewage, pollution survey
item.description: "About 70 percent of households reported some form of respiratory illness and the ones that reported smelling daily odors, or at least four to five times a week, had a higher response rate of having respiratory illnesses and gastrointestinal illnesses.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tesla charging stations in Massachusetts 'intentionally set' on fire, police say
item.description: Seven Tesla superchargers were damaged by a fire in Massachusetts that investigators say was intentionally set, as they look for a suspect.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elissa Slotkin assails Trump's early actions, offers Democrats a way to fight back
item.description: First-term Sen. Elissa Slotkin of Michigan accused President Donald Trump of driving up costs while pushing a giveaway to his billionaire friends.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bill to ban trans athletes from girls' and women's sports teams fails to advance in Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Legislation that aimed to bar transgender women and girls nationwide from participating in school athletic competitions designated for female athletes failed to advance Monday night in a divided Senate as Democrats stood united against an issue that Republicans leveraged in last ye
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Man pleads guilty to murder and attempted murder in shooting at suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill. (AP) — An Illinois man pleaded guilty Monday to killing seven people and injuring dozens more when he opened fire on a 2022 Independence Day parade in a Chicago suburb, a stunning development moments before opening statements in his trial on murder and attempted murder charges.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This rare bean may be your future coffee as warming climate hurts crops
item.description: As leading coffee-producing countries struggle to grow crops in drier, less reliable weather, prices have soared to the highest in decades and the industry is scrambling for solutions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Wyoming's only full-service clinic stops providing abortions after new regulations signed
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyol (AP) — Wyoming's only full-service abortion clinic stopped providing abortions Friday, but it remained open after Gov. Mark Gordon approved a law requiring that such facilities be licensed as surgical centers.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4
